# UC-22.3.4 · DORA Third-Party ICT Provider Concentration Risk

## Description

Quantifies operational dependency on specific cloud providers by measuring API activity concentration across accounts, regions, and services, supporting third-party risk assessments and exit planning.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for DORA compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws, azure and sourcetypes =, aws:cloudtrail, mscs:azure:auditlog. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ingest CloudTrail (org trail) into `index=aws` using AWS TA; (2) ingest Azure Activity via Event Hub using Microsoft Cloud Services TA; (3) create a saved search weekly for procurement/third-party governance dashboards; (4) enrich with cloud account tags via lookup (cost center, vendor name). Sourcetypes: `sourcetype==`, `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`, `sourcetype=mscs:azure:auditlog`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Amazon Web Services (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (eventSource, eventName, awsRegion, userIdentity.arn, recipientAccountId); `index=azure` `sourcetype="mscs:azure:auditlog"` (operationName, resourceProvider, callerIpAddress, ResourceGroup).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest CloudTrail (org trail) into `index=aws` using AWS TA; (2) ingest Azure Activity via Event Hub using Microsoft Cloud Services TA; (3) create a saved search weekly for procurement/third-party governance dashboards; (4) enrich with cloud account tags via lookup (cost center, vendor name).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail") OR (index=azure sourcetype="mscs:azure:auditlog")
| eval provider=if(sourcetype=="aws:cloudtrail", "AWS", "Azure")
| eval service=coalesce(eventSource, resourceProvider)
| eval region=coalesce(awsRegion, ResourceGroup)
| stats count by provider, service, region
| eventstats sum(count) as total
| eval concentration_pct=round(100*count/total, 2)
| sort - concentration_pct
| head 50
| table provider, service, region, count, concentration_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Third-Party ICT Provider Concentration Risk (Art. 28-44)** — Quantifies operational dependency on specific cloud providers by measuring API activity concentration across accounts, regions, and services, supporting third-party risk assessments and exit planning.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (eventSource, eventName, awsRegion, userIdentity.arn, recipientAccountId); `index=azure` `sourcetype="mscs:azure:auditlog"` (operationName, resourceProvider, callerIpAddress, ResourceGroup). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Amazon Web Services (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws, azure; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail, mscs:azure:auditlog. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, index=azure, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **provider** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **service** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **region** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **provider, service, region**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **concentration_pct** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: concentration_pct, count, provider, region, service.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with AWS CloudTrail or the AWS console for the same account, region, and time window, and with IAM or control-tower evidence if this search flags admin activity.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Treemap (share by service), Stacked bar by provider, Table of top (service, region) pairs.

## SPL

```spl
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail") OR (index=azure sourcetype="mscs:azure:auditlog")
| eval provider=if(sourcetype=="aws:cloudtrail", "AWS", "Azure")
| eval service=coalesce(eventSource, resourceProvider)
| eval region=coalesce(awsRegion, ResourceGroup)
| stats count by provider, service, region
| eventstats sum(count) as total
| eval concentration_pct=round(100*count/total, 2)
| sort - concentration_pct
| head 50
| table provider, service, region, count, concentration_pct
```

## Visualization

Treemap (share by service), Stacked bar by provider, Table of top (service, region) pairs.
